name: Indie Hackers
description: A community for founders building profitable, often bootstrapped businesses in public. You create a Product page and share progress through posts and milestones with an audience of indie makers and founders.

auth.notes: Sign up with email/password or Google. After creating a personal account you add a Product page; you can also post updates and milestones from the product.

best_practices: Lead with the build-in-public angle — share real numbers (revenue, users, growth) and lessons; the community values transparency over polish.
Write the tagline as a clear value statement a fellow founder would instantly understand.
Use the 'what it does' / description to explain the problem, your approach, and the business model, not just features.
Pick an accurate product category so the right makers discover and follow it.
Post a launch milestone (e.g. 'Launched!' or first revenue) — milestones surface in the community feed and drive engagement.
Engage genuinely in the forums and other makers' threads; reciprocity is how Indie Hackers visibility works.
Add a square logo and keep the product page updated as you hit new milestones.

questions.label: What does it do?
questions.help: The problem, your approach, and how the business works.

questions.label: Milestone / launch post
questions.help: Optional build-in-public update, e.g. 'We launched!' with context and any metrics.
